content_layers.label: Reader-facing chapter spine
content_layers.canonical_location: ordinary chapter prose outside live-only headings
content_layers.description: The coherent human manuscript layer: problem, insufficiency, mechanism, interfaces, invariants, failure modes, minimum viable implementation, beyond-state-of-the-art endpoint, diagrams, summaries, glossary path, and uncertainty that changes claim meaning.
content_layers.edition_policy: Retained by reader releases and adapted by audio releases. It must remain readable after live-only sections are stripped.

content_layers.label: Live research scaffold
content_layers.canonical_location: per-chapter status, guardrail, test-plan, proof-hook, and source-crosswalk sections
content_layers.description: The live-book machinery that keeps source loading, evidence states, proof scope, validation commands, and overclaiming guardrails visible during research work.
content_layers.edition_policy: Retained by the live book and research releases; stripped or summarized for reader and audio releases.

content_layers.label: Evidence and source matrices
content_layers.canonical_location: appendices, source notes, release records, schemas, proof manifest, and test fixtures
content_layers.description: The audit layer for sources, claim/evidence states, protocol schemas, Codex tests, release records, Corben's authored/supplied sources and local projects, external literature by other authors, changelog, and lineage.
content_layers.edition_policy: Retained by live and research releases. Reader releases keep selected human-useful appendices such as glossary, Corben's sources/local projects, and external literature by other authors while omitting dense matrices unless a major release explicitly includes them.

content_layers.label: Machine-readable contracts
content_layers.canonical_location: book_structure.json, docs/book_outline.md, source inventory, schemas, scripts, Lean modules, and validation commands
content_layers.description: The structured control layer that lets future agents add chapters, route source context, generate proof manifests, validate protocols, and derive editions without hand-renumbering or duplicating manuscripts.
content_layers.edition_policy: Canonical for the live project; excluded from reader and audio manuscripts except where explained in prose.

content_layers.label: Generated release derivatives
content_layers.canonical_location: ignored build workspaces and explicit release artifacts recorded under release_records/
content_layers.description: Generated reader manuscripts, frozen research snapshots, EPUB/PDF/DOCX/HTML builds, audio scripts, MP3/M4B packages, and audio-embedded EPUBs.
content_layers.edition_policy: Never the canonical source of truth. A derivative exists only after generation, review or render, and a release record that names the actual artifact.

content_layers.label: Audio adaptation layer
content_layers.canonical_location: build/audio_script/ and future reviewed audio release workspaces
content_layers.description: The listening-oriented script layer that turns the reviewed reader manuscript into spoken chapters, pronunciation guidance, chapter markers, and companion-note decisions for tables, diagrams, code, and schemas.
content_layers.edition_policy: Derived from the reviewed reader release, not directly from the live book. It does not imply audio files exist until they are generated and checked.

content_layers.label: Reader and audio companion material
content_layers.canonical_location: generated reader/audio release workspaces and explicit release records
content_layers.description: Human-useful notes for diagrams, images, tables, code, schemas, proof-adjacent material, omitted dense matrices, and audio-embedded EPUB packaging checks.
content_layers.edition_policy: Generated downstream of the reader or audio workspace. Companion material helps human consumption, but it is not the canonical evidence ledger and does not imply that an artifact has been published.

reader_spine_contract: Every chapter should remain intelligible from ordinary prose after live-only headings are removed.
Every chapter should preserve both the minimum viable implementation and the beyond-state-of-the-art endpoint in reader and audio derivatives.
Uncertainty, support-state limits, and caveats that change claim meaning must appear in the reader spine, not only in stripped guardrails or source crosswalks.
Images and diagrams that carry meaning should remain in reader releases and receive spoken-treatment notes for audio releases.
Repeated source matrices, proof hooks, Codex test plans, and guardrail blocks belong in the live/research scaffold, not in the bedtime-reading manuscript.
Reader and audio editions are generated from a tagged live-book state; they must not become parallel canonical manuscripts.

release_ladder.gate: Tag a validated live-book commit; the live HTML site remains canonical.
release_ladder.artifact_rule: Only the live Quarto/GitHub Pages book is implied.

release_ladder.gate: Freeze a major-version state with matrices, proof/test status, residuals, and release record.
release_ladder.artifact_rule: Research HTML/PDF artifacts exist only if rendered and recorded.

release_ladder.gate: Generate and manually review build/reader_edition for continuity after live-only scaffolding is stripped.
release_ladder.artifact_rule: Generated source is a candidate manuscript, not an EPUB, PDF, DOCX, or publication.

release_ladder.gate: Render and spot-check each format separately.
release_ladder.artifact_rule: EPUB, DOCX, PDF, HTML, AZW3, MOBI, Markdown, and plain text exist only after successful render or conversion and release-record entry.

release_ladder.gate: Generate a narration-script workspace only from a reviewed reader manuscript.
release_ladder.artifact_rule: Generated scripts, markers, and pronunciation glossary are review inputs, not audio artifacts.

release_ladder.gate: Record, package, spot-check, and document the reviewed script.
release_ladder.artifact_rule: MP3, M4B, and audio-embedded EPUB exist only after generation, packaging checks, and release-record entry.

major_version_policy.version_rule: Every major-version research, reader, or audio release is derived from a tagged live-book commit and recorded in a public-safe release record.
artifact_policy: A profile format is a target, not a produced artifact.
A release record must list only formats that were actually rendered, generated, reviewed, or explicitly marked not attempted.
Generated manuscripts and media builds belong under build/ or another ignored release workspace unless a specific public artifact is intentionally attached elsewhere.
The live book remains the source of truth after a reader or audio release is produced.
Audio embedded in EPUB is a produced artifact only after the exact audio files are embedded, checked, and named in an edition release record.
E-reader convenience formats such as AZW3 or MOBI should be converted only from a reviewed EPUB and recorded as downstream artifacts, not treated as source formats.
Reader releases are for relaxed human reading; audio releases are separate listening artifacts derived from a reviewed reader script, not from the live research scaffold.

companion_material_policy.purpose: Keep human-reader and audiobook companion decisions explicit without making dense live-book scaffolding part of the relaxed reader manuscript.
required_topics: tables that should be summarized rather than read verbatim
code, schemas, and proof-adjacent passages that need companion treatment
omitted source matrices, guardrails, validation details, and release machinery
audio-embedded EPUB packaging and navigation checks
review_requirements: Companion notes must say which dense material was omitted, summarized, retained, or moved.
Meaning-changing uncertainty must stay in the reader or spoken prose, not only in companion notes.
Audio companion notes must be reviewed before MP3, M4B, or audio-embedded EPUB artifacts are claimed.
Reader companion notes must be checked for e-reader usefulness before optional AZW3, MOBI, Markdown, or plain-text derivatives are recorded.
non_claims: Generated companion notes are review aids, not proof of artifact quality.
Generated companion notes do not claim that audio files, EPUB packages, or e-reader conversions exist.

source_sequence: reader format renders or downstream e-reader conversions

bundle_artifact_classes.artifact_rule: Render from the reviewed reader source and record each successful artifact separately.
bundle_artifact_classes.review_gate: Human continuity, typography, figure, diagram, glossary, bibliography, and e-reader checks are complete.

bundle_artifact_classes.artifact_rule: Convert only from a reviewed reader source or reviewed EPUB; never treat these as canonical source formats.
bundle_artifact_classes.review_gate: Open or inspect the converted file enough to confirm navigation, figures, tables, and line wrapping remain usable.

bundle_artifact_classes.artifact_rule: Generate only from a reviewed narration script, then spot-check audio against that script before recording the artifact.
bundle_artifact_classes.review_gate: Pronunciation, chapter markers, metadata, and sample listening checks are complete.

bundle_artifact_classes.artifact_rule: Publish only after the EPUB actually contains the reviewed audio files and opens with playable audio.
bundle_artifact_classes.review_gate: Audio files, navigation, metadata, and companion notes are checked inside the packaged EPUB.

human_quality_gates: The manuscript reads as one continuous book after live-only scaffolding is stripped.
Meaning-critical caveats and uncertainty remain in the reader spine.
Images and diagrams are readable on an e-reader-sized surface or have companion-note treatment.
Dense tables, schemas, proof hooks, source IDs, and release machinery are omitted, summarized, or moved to companion material unless they are essential to the human argument.
The release record names exactly which artifacts exist and which targets remain unattempted, failed, or not applicable.
audio_quality_gates: The audio script is reviewed after the reader manuscript review, not generated directly from the live book.
Tables, diagrams, images, code, schemas, and proof-adjacent passages have reviewed spoken summaries or companion-note references.
Pronunciation, chapter markers, metadata, and listening spot checks are recorded before MP3, M4B, or audio-embedded EPUB is claimed.
Audio embedded in EPUB is verified inside the EPUB package before being listed as produced.

reader_manuscript_policy.reader_facing_source: Chapter prose outside strip_headings is the human-readable spine used to derive reader editions.
retain_section_intent: core claim prose with uncertainty preserved
glossary, Corben's sources/local projects, and external literature by other authors
continuity_requirements: Do not put a caveat that changes claim meaning only inside a stripped live-only section.
Avoid repeating source crosswalk or guardrail language in the reader-facing spine.
Before a major reader release, review the generated manuscript for chapter-to-chapter continuity, duplicate explanation, missing transitions, and e-reader typography.
human_reader_quality_floor: The generated reader manuscript should feel like a continuous book, not an exported research notebook.
Chapter openings and summaries should carry transitions after live-only scaffolding is stripped.
Tables, schemas, and proof-adjacent material should remain only when they help a human reader; otherwise move detail to live or companion material.
Images and diagrams should be readable on an e-reader-sized viewport or have a companion route for audio.
The reader release should preserve uncertainty and evidence limits without repeating source-management machinery in every chapter.
ebook_quality_checks: EPUB opens in at least one e-reader application or device with readable tables, figures, and diagrams.
PDF render is attempted only when local PDF dependencies are available and is checked for page breaks, figure sizing, and table overflow.
DOCX opens with headings, figures, tables, and bibliography in usable form.
Reader HTML is spot-checked for stripped live-only scaffolding, navigation, and image paths.
Images and diagrams remain readable in the reader artifact or have a clear companion-note route for audio.
Any optional AZW3, MOBI, Markdown, or plain-text derivative is generated from the reviewed reader source or reviewed EPUB and listed separately in the release record.
optional_downstream_formats: mobi or azw3 converted from a reviewed EPUB with an external tool

required_reader_headings: Beyond the State of the Art
reader_spine_validation.purpose: Derive the reader edition and verify that every generated chapter retains a substantial human-readable spine, section-level prose substance, and chapter-specific Handoff continuity after live-only scaffolding is stripped.

reader_evidence_boundary_validation.purpose: Derive the reader edition and verify that every live source chapter preserves a raw core-claim marker, while every generated reader chapter strips that machine marker and repeated support boilerplate but preserves the claim text plus a compact plain-language support-state boundary in the Core Claim section.

live_human_view_policy.human_view_core_claim_marker_policy: hide raw bracketed core-claim markers in Human view while preserving claim text and support-state prose
live_human_view_policy.human_view_support_boundary_policy: replace repeated AI/research support-state boilerplate in Human view with a compact evidence-boundary phrase while preserving the original sentence in AI view
live_human_view_policy.human_view_section_number_policy: hide rendered section numbers in Human view to avoid numbering gaps left by stripped live-only sections and unheaded bridge prose
live_human_view_policy.reader_release_processing: Reader generation strips .asi-ai-only fenced div blocks, unwraps .asi-human-only fenced div blocks as unheaded prose, and strips the live-only headings listed in reader_release.strip_headings.
live_human_view_policy.chapter_contract: Every chapter's ordinary prose must remain reader-facing. Optional .asi-human-only blocks may provide chapter-specific human prose digests; optional .asi-ai-only or .asi-live-only blocks must not carry meaning-critical caveats absent from the reader spine.

audio_manuscript_policy.audio_in_epub_rule: An audio-embedded EPUB is a produced artifact only after the EPUB actually contains the reviewed audio files and the release record says so.
review_requirements: Generate or write a narration script from the reviewed reader edition.
Convert tables, schemas, code, and diagrams into spoken summaries or companion-note references.
Maintain a pronunciation glossary for ASI Stack terms, source IDs, and invented architecture labels.
Spot-check audio against the reviewed script before listing MP3, M4B, or audio-embedded EPUB as produced.
Confirm an audio-embedded EPUB actually contains the reviewed audio files before listing it as produced.
audio_packaging_checks: Chapter markers match final audio timecodes.
Audio metadata includes title, major version, source tag or commit, narrator/tooling note, and license or rights statement.
Companion notes cover diagrams, tables, schemas, proof hooks, and omitted dense matrices.
Sample listening checks cover the preface, one early chapter, one technical chapter, one appendix or notes segment, and the ending.
An audio-embedded EPUB is opened after packaging to confirm the audio files are present and playable.
spoken_treatment_rules: Do not read dense source matrices, schemas, or proof tables verbatim unless the reader edition intentionally keeps them.
Turn diagrams into short verbal walkthroughs only when the diagram carries meaning not already explained in prose.
Move low-level citations, source IDs, and release metadata to chapter-end notes or companion material unless they change the claim boundary.
Preserve uncertainty and evidence limits in spoken prose whenever omission would overstate a claim.

audiences.label: AIs and writing agents
needs: stable chapter ids and manifest ordering
guardrail notes that prevent accidental overclaiming

audiences.label: Human researchers
needs: appendices for audit, reproduction, and contribution
known residuals and blocked evidence transitions

audiences.label: Interested human readers
needs: clean typography for e-readers and bedtime reading
images and diagrams that explain the architecture
bibliography and further reading without live-workflow clutter
no repeated source matrix, guardrail, or test-plan scaffolding in every chapter

profiles.label: Live Book
profiles.purpose: Canonical public Quarto site for the living architecture, source mapping, evidence discipline, proof hooks, schemas, validation records, and research workflow.
profiles.source_policy: All tracked book source remains in the main repo. Raw source exports stay local/private unless explicitly approved for publication.
non_claims: The live book is not automatically final-draft prose.
The live book does not claim a proof, benchmark, source-derived support state, or audiobook unless the artifact exists and the validation result is recorded.

profiles.label: Research Release
profiles.purpose: Frozen major-version snapshot for review, audit, citation, and replication work while preserving the live evidence and source machinery.
profiles.source_policy: Derived from a tagged commit. Keeps matrices, evidence appendices, schema/proof appendices, release records, and known residuals.
release_gate: live_book gate passes at the tagged commit
release record lists residuals and non-claims
PDF render is attempted only when local PDF dependencies are available
non_claims: A research release is a frozen review artifact, not a promise that all claims are source-derived or empirically proved.
PDF output must not be reported unless Quarto actually renders it.

profiles.label: Reader Release
profiles.purpose: Major-version human manuscript for e-readers and relaxed reading, derived from the living book while removing AI/research workflow scaffolding.
profiles.source_policy: Generated from tracked Quarto source at a tagged commit. The generator strips live-only sections by heading name and keeps the narrative, diagrams, glossary, Corben's sources/local projects path, and external-literature-by-other-authors path.
release_gate: reader manuscript is manually reviewed for narrative continuity
PDF render passes only when PDF dependencies are available
reader release record states which formats were actually rendered
non_claims: The reader edition is not the source of truth for evidence state, proof scope, or source queues.
The reader edition should not contain raw source exports, private notes, or unreconciled guardrail scaffolding.
Audiobook output is a separate audio release artifact, not implied by EPUB or DOCX generation.

profiles.label: Audio Release
profiles.purpose: Narration-ready version of a major reader release, optimized for listening rather than table-heavy technical audit.
profiles.source_policy: Derived from the reader release after human manuscript review. Tables, schemas, code blocks, proof hooks, and dense crosswalks are converted to concise narration or moved to companion notes.
narration_rules: Replace tables with short spoken summaries unless the table is central to the argument.
Read diagrams as verbal descriptions only when they add understanding.
Move citations and source caveats to chapter-end notes rather than interrupting the argument.
Maintain a pronunciation glossary for ASI Stack terms and source IDs.
Do not synthesize or publish audio unless the exact script has been reviewed for human consumption.
release_gate: reader_release gate passes for the same major version
narration script exists and is reviewed
audio files are generated and spot-checked
chapter markers and metadata are verified
embedded EPUB audio is reported only if the EPUB artifact actually includes the audio
non_claims: The audio release is not an evidence ledger.
Audio narration must not silently omit uncertainty that changes the meaning of a claim.
